title: Serratula lanceolata
intro: Serratula lanceolata, also known as Lance-leaf Milk Thistle, is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the Asteraceae family. Traditionally, it has been used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues and liver problems, due to its potential anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and hepatoprotective properties. The bioactive constituents of Serratula lanceolata include flavonoids, such as silymarin, and other phenolic compounds that contribute to its medicinal effects. In herbal medicine, Serratula lanceolata is commonly used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are believed to support liver health and alleviate symptoms of liver and gallbladder disorders.
taxonomy: Serratula lanceolata is a member of the dandelion tribe in the family Asteraceae. This classification places it within the larger group of flowering plants known as angiosperms. It belongs to the order Asterales and the subclass Asteridae. The family Asteraceae includes thousands of species and is one of the largest families of flowering plants. Serratula lanceolata is native to the mountainous regions of Europe, Asia, and Africa.
